region_of_origin: Global — freshwater species found across Europe, North America, and Asia

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical single serving of raw perch is approximately 100 g, equivalent to one medium boneless fillet, before any cooking preparation.

nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Primary values sourced from Singapore HPB Food Composition Database (cr_id F03010026, confidence 0.95). Cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for perch, mixed species, raw (FDC ID 175168). Values are consistent between both approved sources. Saturated fat (0.19 g/100 g) sourced from USDA SR Legacy as HPB does not report fatty acid fractions separately.

gi_source.title: N/A — food contains negligible carbohydrates; GI not applicable for raw perch
gi_source.source_match_notes: Glycemic index is not applicable for raw perch as this food contains negligible carbohydrates (0.0 g/100 g). The numeric value 0 is used as a sentinel consistent with the template default for not-applicable GI; gi_category is set to 'not_applicable' and gi_source documents the rationale. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 by definition.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (62 mg/100 g) and high potassium (269 mg/100 g) yielding a favourable sodium-to-potassium ratio consistent with DASH dietary principles
hypertension.guidance: Raw perch is an excellent protein choice for hypertensive individuals; its low sodium content and high potassium level actively support blood pressure regulation, provided it is prepared without added salt, soy sauce, or high-sodium marinades.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrate and zero sugar with high lean protein (19.4 g/100 g) that supports glycaemic stability and satiety
type2_diabetes.guidance: Raw perch has no glycaemic impact and its high-quality lean protein slows gastric emptying, reducing post-prandial glucose excursions; individuals with type 2 diabetes should avoid battered, breaded, or sweet-glazed preparations that add refined carbohydrates.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Very low total fat (0.92 g/100 g), low saturated fat (0.19 g/100 g), and presence of omega-3 polyunsaturated fatty acids (0.33 g/100 g) with moderate cholesterol (90 mg/100 g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Raw perch is a heart-healthy lean protein source with minimal saturated fat and beneficial omega-3 fatty acids; it is suitable for cardiovascular disease management when prepared by steaming, baking, or grilling rather than deep-frying, and should be paired with fatty fish such as salmon or mackerel twice weekly to maximise omega-3 intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Perch (raw) refers to fresh, uncooked fillets of freshwater perch, a widely distributed fish species encompassing yellow perch (Perca flavescens) native to North America and European perch (Perca fluviatilis) found across Europe and Asia. The raw flesh is white to pinkish in colour with a firm, flaky texture and a mild, slightly sweet flavour. Perch is a lean white fish with exceptionally low total fat (0.92 g/100 g) and very low saturated fat (0.19 g/100 g), making it one of the leanest freshwater fish available. It delivers high-quality complete protein at 19.4 g per 100 g, supplying all essential amino acids. Carbohydrate content is zero, making it suitable for low-carbohydrate, ketogenic, and diabetic dietary patterns. Sodium is low at 62 mg/100 g while potassium is relatively high at 269 mg/100 g, supporting a favourable sodium-to-potassium ratio beneficial for blood pressure management. Calcium content is notable at 80 mg/100 g for a fish fillet, and cholesterol is moderate at 90 mg/100 g. Perch provides B-vitamins including B12 (1.9 mcg/100 g), niacin, B6, and minerals including phosphorus, magnesium, zinc, and selenium. Clinically, raw perch is appropriate for individuals managing hypertension, type 2 diabetes, and cardiovascular disease. It is naturally gluten-free, dairy-free, and halal. Common preparations include pan-frying, baking, steaming, grilling, and poaching. The glycemic index is not applicable as the food contains negligible carbohydrates.
common_user_queries: How much protein is in perch?
Is perch good for high blood pressure?
Is perch good for heart health?
What are the nutrients in raw perch?

confidence_reason: Primary nutrient values sourced directly from Singapore HPB Food Composition Database (cr_id F03010026, confidence 0.95) and cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for perch, mixed species, raw. Both approved sources are in close agreement. Fatty acid fractions and micronutrients supplemented from USDA SR Legacy where HPB does not report them. No estimation or analogy required for core macros.
notes: Carbohydrate_g is naturally zero as perch is a pure animal protein and fat food source with no carbohydrate content; confirmed zero per Singapore HPB Food Composition Database and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy. Sugar_g is naturally zero for the same reason. Dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as fish contains no plant-derived fibre. Trans_fat_g is reported as 0.0 (confirmed negligible per USDA SR Legacy). Vitamin_c_mg set to -1: not reported in HPB or USDA SR Legacy for raw perch. Vitamin_a_mcg_rae set to -1: not reported in HPB or USDA SR Legacy for raw perch. Folate_mcg_dfe set to -1: not reported in HPB database; USDA SR Legacy reports trace amounts below reliable detection threshold for this species. Vitamin_d_mcg set to -1: not reported in HPB; USDA SR Legacy does not provide a reliable value for perch specifically. Vitamin_e_mg set to -1: not reported in either HPB or USDA SR Legacy for this species. Glycemic index is set to the template numeric sentinel value 0 with gi_category 'not_applicable'; raw perch contains 0.0 g/100 g carbohydrate and GI is not applicable per the critical rule for negligible-carbohydrate foods — the validator requires a numeric value in this field. Rheumatoid arthritis health suitability from input record noted but not carried forward to health_context as the three mandatory conditions (Hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, Cardiovascular Disease) are required; RA may be added to additional_conditions in a future enrichment pass. FoodEx2 code A00LC assigned as the closest defensible freshwater fish raw category code; reviewer should validate against the current FoodEx2 master catalogue. SNOMED CT omitted as no confirmed concept code for raw perch was identified in publicly available SNOMED CT browser as of 2026-04-16.

veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: Record passes all six hard gates and scores 8/8 on the Q1–Q8 scorecard. The previous iteration set glycemic_index to null which the validator rejected as non-numeric. This iteration restores glycemic_index to the numeric sentinel value 0 (consistent with the RESULT template default) while gi_category remains 'not_applicable' and gi_source and notes fully document that GI is not applicable for this negligible-carbohydrate food. Primary nutrient data sourced from Singapore HPB Food Composition Database (cr_id F03010026, confidence 0.95) and cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for perch, mixed species, raw. Zero carbohydrate, sugar, and fibre values are justified as naturally zero for a pure animal protein food. All -1 sentinel values are documented. Ontology is honest with no invented codes. Health context covers all three mandatory conditions with specific clinical guidance. Aliases are consistent between flat array and structured object. Multilingual naming rules are satisfied as perch is a generic Western/global food item not requiring mandatory non-English names.
